title: [InstCombine] Generalize and consolidate phi translation check
summary:
The developers generalized by moving the phi-translatability check from a restrictive pre-filter (`canSelectOperandBeMappingIntoPredBlock`) into the core transformation (`foldOpIntoPhi`), enabling more cases to be optimized. The key insight was that dominance suffices: if an operand dominates the phi block, it requires no translation and is always safe. This replaces overly conservative heuristics that blocked valid transforms when operands were defined outside the phi block.

The patch simplifies the logic: it now attempts the fold for any select with a phi condition, deferring checks until inside `foldOpIntoPhi`. There, it validates each operand using precise dominance analysis, allowing constants, arguments, and any instruction dominating the phi block. This captures the original test case (where `%a` and `%b` dominate the phi block) and extends to other similar patterns.

**Actionable takeaway**: When generalizing pattern-based optimizations, push precise safety checks (like dominance) into the core transformation logic instead of early conservative filters, enabling more opportunities while maintaining correctness.
